Soapwort (Saponaria officinalis) is a perennial plant species that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its soap-like properties and is a source of saponins, which have been used for their emulsifying and surfactant properties.

Soapwort has been used to treat skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

The bioactive constituents of Soapwort include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Soapwort is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and creams, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other skin issues.
